title: misconfigured_storage
description: An S3 bucket containing sensitive customer PII is discovered to be publicly accessible, with evidence it has been indexed by external search engines.

timeline.title: Public Bucket Discovered
timeline.description: A security researcher reports via your bug bounty programme that an S3 bucket containing customer PII is publicly accessible and indexed by Google.

timeline.title: Exposure Confirmed
timeline.description: Security team confirms the S3 bucket has 'Public' ACL. It contains 180,000 customer records including names, addresses, and payment card details.

timeline.title: External Indexing Evident
timeline.description: Google cache and web archive services have indexed directory listings and sample files. The data has been publicly accessible for an estimated 6 weeks.

timeline.title: Bucket Made Private
timeline.description: S3 bucket ACL set to private. Public access blocked at account level. Bucket removed from external indices via removal requests.

timeline.title: Scope Assessment Complete
timeline.description: All exposed files catalogued. Regulatory notification obligations assessed. Affected customers identified for notification.

timeline.title: Controls Implemented
timeline.description: S3 Block Public Access enabled account-wide. AWS Config rule monitoring for public buckets deployed. Data classification applied to all storage.

node-1.title: Publicly Accessible S3 Bucket with PII
node-1.situation: A security researcher has reported that your S3 bucket 'prod-customer-data' is publicly accessible. It contains 180,000 customer records including names, addresses, and payment card details. Google has indexed it.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Immediately set the bucket ACL to private and enable S3 Block Public Access at the account level to prevent any re-exposure.

options.label: B
options.text: First, verify the researcher's report by accessing the bucket yourself before making any changes.

options.label: C
options.text: Delete the bucket to remove the exposure, then restore data from backup once a private bucket is created.

node-2.title: Assess Duration and Scope of Exposure
node-2.situation: The bucket is now private. S3 access logs show the bucket has been publicly accessible for 6 weeks. You need to determine who accessed it and what data was exposed to inform breach notifications.
node-2.question: How do you assess the exposure scope?

options.label: A
options.text: Review S3 server access logs and CloudTrail for anonymous (unauthenticated) access events over the 6-week window, and catalogue all exposed files to identify the full scope of PII involved.

options.label: B
options.text: Assume the data was not accessed because the researcher was the first to report it and proceed without reviewing access logs.

node-3.title: Regulatory Notification and Customer Communication
node-3.situation: Access logs show numerous anonymous downloads over the 6-week period. Payment card data was exposed — PCI DSS breach notification obligations apply in addition to GDPR requirements.
node-3.question: How do you handle regulatory and customer notifications?

options.label: A
options.text: Notify your Payment Card Industry QSA, submit a breach report to your supervisory authority within GDPR timelines, notify affected customers, and reward the security researcher via your bug bounty programme.

options.label: B
options.text: Only notify the data protection authority and skip PCI DSS notification since payment card data was only one part of the exposure.

node-4.title: Prevent Misconfigured Storage Recurrence
node-4.situation: Notifications are underway. Now you must ensure no other S3 buckets across your AWS estate are misconfigured, and implement ongoing detection.
node-4.question: What controls do you implement to prevent recurrence?

options.label: A
options.text: Enable S3 Block Public Access at the AWS Organisation level, deploy AWS Config rules to auto-remediate public buckets, classify all data in S3 using Macie, and conduct a full cloud storage inventory audit.

options.label: B
options.text: Enable S3 Block Public Access on only the exposed bucket and rely on manual audits for all others.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The publicly accessible S3 bucket was secured immediately. The full exposure scope was determined from access logs, regulatory and PCI DSS notifications were made, and organisation-wide S3 Block Public Access with AWS Config auto-remediation now prevents future storage misconfigurations.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
